title: anachortus divaricatus subsp. macrantherus
intro: Anachortus divaricatus subsp. macrantherus, also known as the large-flowered winter aconite, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family, characterized by its yellow flowers and toxic properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Anachortus divaricatus subsp. macrantherus include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its use in treating certain skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make tinctures, infusions, and ointments, but caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential for adverse reactions.
